objective: Review the HELLO-BUG-002 fix plan: guard change if shout and not formal -> if shout, plus a standalone CliRunner regression script, against triage and actual hello.py code.
key_changes: hello.py:15 guard if shout and not formal: -> if shout: (restores HEAD; net hello.py diff empty)
New standalone regression script tests/test_shout_formal.py run via python3 tests/test_shout_formal.py (pytest not installed, click 8.1.6)
findings: Verified via git diff: HEAD has correct if shout:, working tree reintroduced the bug; restore yields empty net hello.py diff as the plan claims.
Empirically confirmed all 4 expected strings: formal+shout=GREETINGS, ALICE (no trailing !), informal shout=HELLO, ALICE!, formal=Greetings, Alice, formal+shout+count2 repeats twice.
No regression: fix only adds formal-shout branch; informal-shout and --count paths untouched (count-2 case verified, no FORGE-BUG-007 regression).
Regression guard discriminates: buggy working-tree guard yields Greetings, Alice and fails the primary assertion, so the test is genuine not tautological.
Test form correct: standalone python3 script with non-zero exit on failure, not pytest; matches null config.commands.test and absent tests/ dir.
Advisory (non-blocking): plan says loader imports the 'sibling' hello.py, but with test at tests/, hello.py is the parent dir; run command resolves via cwd, implement phase should resolve robustly.
